Liabilities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of
the reciprocal accounts under the three funds and book the necessary adjustments
to bring their balances into an agreement. The Municipal Accountant should
closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these
are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error or omission.
Moreover, whenever possible, settle immediately transactions between funds to
minimize efforts in monitoring and reconciliation and more importantly avoid
erroneous account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the balances
related to the PPE account as a change in accounting policy and, subsequently, to
record depreciation based on a five percent residual value in compliance with
PAG 6 of PPSAS No. 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment. We also recommended
that subsidiary ledgers be maintained for all PPE accounts to ensure the accuracy
and reliability of their recorded book value.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Auditor the
following documents to substantiate the grant of COVID-19 Hazard Pay:

a) Lacking approved DTRs;
b) Inter-Agency Task Force for the Management of Emerging Infectious
Diseases Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace; and
c) Authority from the head of agency/office to render service during the
period of the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as well
as the entitlement of the COVID-19 Hazard Pay.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. strictly adhere to the provisions of the Omnibus Rules on Leave and
CSC Resolution No. 34 on the granting and approval of monetization
of leave credits;
b. meticulously check, reconcile and monitor the employees’ leave cards
to reflect the correct leave credit balances; and
c. ensure that the documentary requirements for monetization of leave
credits as required under COA No. 2012-001 are properly complied.

We recommended that the irregular procedure in the grant of cash
advances, such as the grant of multiple cash advances to the DO – Designate in
one day be immediately stopped.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint or designate an
additional DO to avoid the granting of multiple cash advances to the incumbent
DO - Designate.

We recommended that Management require accountable officers handling
government funds and property to post bond in the amount corresponding to their
total cash and property accountability in order to protect the interest of the
Municipality.

Henceforth, no Accountable Officer shall be allowed to perform their
duties and responsibilities with expired fidelity bonds and ensure that cash
advances granted to accountable officers do not exceed the maximum
accountability covered by their fidelity bonds.

In addition, submit a copy of the Confirmation Letter from the Bureau of
the Treasury to the Auditor for information and filing.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be instructed to fast-
track the preparation of the financial statements and submit them to our office at
the soonest possible time.

We further recommended that Management closely monitor the strict
adherence to the requirements of pertinent laws and regulations relating to the
submission of financial statements and other accounting reports, and cause the
suspension of payment of salaries of employees responsible for the delayed
submission or non-submission until the same shall be complied.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all
department heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished
AAPSI and assign a personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the
Auditor at the soonest possible time, and within 60 days upon receipt of the
AARs for the succeeding years.

We further recommended and Management also agreed that pertinent
documents to support the actions taken should also be submitted to facilitate our
validation of the responses.

We recommended that Management desist from charging ineligible expenditures from the BGCM, and to make sure that only expenses allowed under DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 are charged thereto.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Not Implemented Some RDS were submitted which served as partial monitoring of the repacked relief goods and distributed. Lastly, the Municipal Accountant did not record all purchases according to COA circulars.
observations.reason: Sound internal control mechanism had not been established due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the irregular procedure in the grant of cash advances, such as the grant of multiple cash advances to the DO – Designate in one day be immediately stopped. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint or designate an additional DO to avoid the granting of multiple cash advances to the incumbent DO - Designate.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Not Implemented Management commented that in its aim to give immediate service, cash advances were drawn whenever disbursement vouchers (DVs) were forwarded from the Municipal Accounting Office to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO). To prevent the grant of multiple cash advances to its DO, the MTO will therefore establish a cutoff period for the submission of the DVs for issuance of cash advance, the payout periods, and the liquidation of the said cash advances. With these procedures to be established, Management did not see the need to appoint or designate additional DO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require accountable officers handling government funds and property to post bond in the amount corresponding to their total cash and property accountability in order to protect the interest of the Municipality. Henceforth, no Accountable Officer shall be allowed to perform their duties and responsibilities with expired fidelity bonds and ensure that cash advances granted to accountable officers do not exceed the maximum accountability covered by their fidelity bonds. In addition, submit a copy of the Confirmation Letter from the Bureau of the Treasury to the Auditor for information and filing.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Not Implemented A copy of the Confirmation Letter for the Fidelity Bond of the DO-Designate was already submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be instructed to fast-track the preparation of the financial statements and submit them to our office at the soonest possible time. We further recommended that Management closely monitor the strict adherence to the requirements of pertinent laws and regulations relating to the submission of financial statements and other accounting reports, and cause the suspension of payment of salaries of employees responsible for the delayed submission or non-submission until the same shall be complied.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Not Implemented Management commented that the delay in the submission of the CY 2021 financial statements was mainly due to the onslaught of Typhoon Odette that greatly affected the Municipality. To meet the February 14 deadline, a cut-off for obligating disbursements will be set so that the Municipal Accounting Office will have a feasible working schedule for preparing the required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all department heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished AAPSI and assign a personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the Auditor at the soonest possible time, and within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for the succeeding years. We further recommended and Management also agreed that pertinent documents to support the actions taken should also be submitted to facilitate our validation of the responses.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Not Implemented The Audit Team sent Audit Query No. Manjuyod-2022-001 through email requesting for the submission of the accomplished AAPSI by March 15, 2022. It was observed that in the previous year, the AAPSI submitted by Management was not properly accomplished because only the status of implementation was specified. The corresponding actions taken/actions to be taken were not clearly stated while the reasons for partial/delay/non-implementation were usually not explained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management advise the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain the Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations. The format of these forms can be referred to in Annexes 13, 14, 15 and 16 of Volume II of the MNGAS. At the back of these forms are instructions on how it shall be accomplished.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Not Implemented The Municipal Budget Officer had already prepared RAAO for CY 2021. The RAAO signifying that the RAAO was not utilized properly as a monitoring tool. Furthermore, some obligations exceeded their appropriations signifying that the RAAO was not utilized properly as a monitoring tool.
observations.reason: The RAAO was prepared but not utilized properly as a monitoring tool; some obligations exceeded their appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. immediately stop utilizing the LDRRMF for PPAs which are not properly chargeable to the Fund; b. ensure that the PPAs to be included in the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan should conform to the PPAs enumerated in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; c. the LDRRMF Investment Plan be prepared in adherence to the format prescribed under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and d. the amount of ₱600,425.00 representing a portion of the LDRRMF appropriations spent for expenses not properly chargeable thereto, should be refunded from the appropriations of the GF Proper.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Not Implemented No refund was provided for the LDRRMF. The funds were still charged to the LDRRMF and some appropriations were utilized for non-chargeable PPAs. The ML had already issued memorandums ordering settlement and reclassification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, contrary to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, and submit the report to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Not Implemented The LDRRM Officer prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit the report to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. The report was not properly reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order the withholding of payment of any money due to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances thirty days after the service of the demand letters where no liquidation has been submitted by the accountable officers concerned, pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002. We further recommended that Management desist from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify cash advances pertaining to travelling expenses to the Advances to Officers and Employees account (Account 1-03-05-040), instead of reporting these amounts under the Due from Officers and Employees account (Account 1-03-06-020).
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Not Implemented The Municipal Mayor had already issued a memorandum order dated August 24, 2020 for the settlement of the unliquidated cash advances. Second ML demand letters had also been sent by the Municipal Accountant to the concerned employees in 2021. Some accounts were already fully settled as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and Social Workers support their claims for hazard pay with proof of actual exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks, certifications from appropriate agencies and the documents required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in order to establish validity of payments.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Not Implemented The MSWDO provided a written explanation which formed part of the CY 2020 AAR. Only the list of SAP recipients was submitted but the lacking documents enumerated in the Annexes were excluded. For CY 2021, some supporting documents were still not submitted as specified under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management henceforth stop the utilization of GAD funds for programs, projects and activities which do not clearly address gender issues and concerns, and instead endeavor to strictly comply with Section 36 of RA No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to ensure that the programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Not Implemented Programs, projects, and activities which did not clearly address gender issues and concerns were still included in the CY 2020 GAD Plan and some utilizations were still made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, evaluate the feasibility, prioritize the implementation of the different infrastructure projects of the Municipality in accordance with the approved development plan as required under Section 476 (4) of R.A. No. 7160. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor evaluate the number of personnel assigned in the Office of the Municipal Engineer and consider the appointment of new personnel or the detail of personnel from other offices to the MEO. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Development Council review projects funded by continuing appropriations in order to determine projects which have already become irrelevant and to recommend reversion and realignment thereof.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Not Implemented Some projects were implemented by contract instead of by administration to expedite their implementation. For CY 2021, none of the 23 projects programmed under the 20% DF were completed. The Municipal development council had not fully reviewed projects funded by continuing appropriations.
observations.reason: The preferred mode of implementation and lack of engineering personnel and accumulated backlog impeded timely completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal DRRMO prioritize the implementation of programs, projects and activities under the 70% Mitigation Fund which should be aligned with the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan of 2011-2028, in consideration of the indicators provided therein and to regularly monitor its implementation to ensure the municipal government’s resilience to disaster.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Not Implemented The LDRRM Officer was also the OIC General Services Officer, preventing him from focusing on the monitoring of the implementation of the aforementioned fund. Implementation of the 70% mitigation fund PPAs was still being monitored and some programs were prioritized.
observations.reason: The LDRRM Officer was also assigned other duties (OIC GS), limiting focus on monitoring LDRRMF implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMF be reimbursed for the cost of 163 sacks of rice distributed to victims of two armed clashes between government troops and insurgents and to charge the expenses to Appropriations for Peace and Order. We further recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Disaster Risk Management Council determine whether all requisites and circumstances for the declaration of a state of calamity as required in R.A. No. 10121 and the NDRRMC MC Order No. 60, s. 2019 have been complied and appropriate reports prepared to support their recommendation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Not Implemented SB Resolution No. 046 series of 2015 had been enacted allocating funds for Peace and Order. Personnel concerned were still needed to be knowledgeable with RA 10121 requirements and the NDRRMC MC Order No. 60, s. 2019. The Municipality still needed to prepare appropriate reports to support their recommendation.
observations.reason: The concerned personnel still needed to be knowledgeable with the technical procedures and guidelines of RA 10121.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor exclude the barangay from the assessment roll and remit collections to the appropriate municipality. We further recommended that the Municipal Assessor continually update the assessment roll on the basis of the assessment roll of newly-assessed/reassessed real properties prepared by the Municipal Assessor. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Assessor write a letter to the Provincial Assessor regarding the exclusion of Brgy. Cabcaban from the assessment roll of the Municipality of Manjuyod since Brgy. Cabcaban is a barangay of the Municipality of Bindoy.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Not Implemented The basis for the set-up was the Certified List of Taxpayers and Amounts Due and Collectible. This was also the basis made for the RPT set-up of RPT/SET set-up of RPT for CY 2021. Barangay Cabcaban was included in the assessment roll of the Municipality of Manjuyod. The Municipal Assessor had already planned to write to the Provincial Assessor regarding the exclusion of Brgy. Cabcaban from the assessment roll.
observations.reason: The Barangay Cabcaban assessment roll issue was still pending resolution; the Municipal Assessor planned to write to the Provincial Assessor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from using the 20% Development Fund for expenditures not among the allowable uses thereof as enumerated in the updated guidelines and instead, utilize the fund for priority development projects which are aligned with the Philippine Development Plan and the Public Investment Program, in accordance with DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. We also recommended that labor payrolls be supported with Programs of Work (POW) and Accomplishment Reports (AR) and that the abovementioned expenditures be charged to the General Fund Proper.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Not Implemented The Municipal Engineer, Municipal Mayor and concerned offices were coordinating the necessary documents. The payrolls and purchases still needed to be supported with Accomplishment reports. Labor payrolls were still being charged to the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Submit the respective Programs of Work (POW) and Accomplishment Reports (AR) for the job order personnel charged to infrastructure projects; b. Henceforth, support all vouchers and payrolls covering disbursements for projects with POWs and ARs; and c. Desist from charging to the 20% DF the wages of JOs assigned to do administrative work in various offices of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Not Implemented The Municipal Engineer had already attached POWs and ARs for some projects. However, not all payrolls were supported by Accomplishment Reports. The lacking POWs and ARs for the respective personnel charged to the projects were not submitted. Some wages of JO personnel performing administrative work were still charged to the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal GAD Officer to establish and maintain a GAD database in order to provide the data necessary to properly plan GAD projects and activities. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish a gender-responsive Monitoring and Evaluation System in order that GAD projects and activities will be properly planned and implemented and its results assessed to ensure the success of the programs.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Not Implemented The MSWDO had prepared GAD program and some activities were undertaken. However, a GAD database had not yet been established. The MSWDO had prepared the GAD Plan and allocations for the same were made in the 2022 GAD Budget according to the MSWDO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management monitor the completion thereof by the Municipal Engineer's office so that the building can finally be used for its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Not Implemented The crisis center was completed and fully functional as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Prioritize the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003. In the meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame; b. Enforce strictly the Municipality's ordinances relative to solid waste management and ensure 100 percent compliance thereof; c. Involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of segregation at source in accordance with R.A. No. 9003; and d. Take steps to revitalize the operations of the waterworks system so that it can finally become a viable economic enterprise as it was meant to be.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Not Implemented The MENR Officer had conducted Solid Waste Management trainings in all barangays. The Municipality had drafted a Memorandum of Agreement with a privately-owned sanitary landfill for the proposed site. The MENRO had conducted initial evaluation and the proposed site evaluation by the DENR was pending. The LGU was still awaiting the outcome of the evaluation and the MENR still needed to proceed with the implementation of a sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: There was difficulty in allocating the Municipality’s budget for the establishment of a sanitary landfill; MENRO lacked capacity and trainings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal treasurer request the treasurer of the respective leagues to acknowledge their payments (as well as collections from other member-municipalities) with government accountable receipts in order that the funds may be subject to government accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Not Implemented The municipal vice mayor and municipal mayor were requested regarding the mentioned payments but the leagues had not issued official receipts. The municipal treasurer requested that official receipts be issued by the respective leagues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately stop the use of the rescue vehicles for ordinary, day-to-day operations in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles to times of actual calamity. The Municipal Mayor should prioritize the use of the vehicles solely for rescue and response activities and ensure they are on standby for times of actual calamity.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Not Implemented The LDRRM vehicles had been utilized for purposes other than LDRRM needs because of the lack of serviceable vehicles. The vehicles were still being utilized for daily operations. The LDRRMF had stipulated guidelines to restrict the use of such vehicles.
observations.reason: Lack of serviceable vehicles forced the use of LDRRM vehicles for other purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer monitor collections of each special account and disburse only according to the availability of funds. Reconciliations should be prepared and the necessary adjustments should be made to correct the cash balances as of the reporting date.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Not Implemented The Accounting and Budget office were in the process of preparing the necessary data for the transfer of funds. The budget officer was only hired in November 2021. The municipal accountant was preparing reconciliations and coordinating with the Budget office.
observations.reason: The new budget officer was hired only in November 2021; reconciliations were in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all employees concerned to refund the overpayment and henceforth observe strictly the applicable regulations on the grant of any fringe benefits.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Not Implemented The Municipality had already sent demand letters to the recipients of the Productivity Enhancement Incentive but none was returned. The Municipality was still pursuing the recovery of the overpayment.
observations.reason: The Municipality was still waiting for decision on appeal related to the disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the present administration initiate effort to recover the donated assets. The Municipal Mayor had already requested the Municipal Treasurer to initiate recovery of the donated properties. Management should resume efficient and effective day-to-day operations, maintain the efficient delivery of basic services, and maintain the Municipality’s tourist attraction, the White Sand Bar.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Not Implemented The Municipal Mayor had already requested recovery of the donated assets. However, most recipients refused to return the items and some donated items were destroyed (marina cottage razed by fire). The Municipality demanding for the return had not yet been successful.
observations.reason: Most recipients refused to return the donated items; one donated property was razed by fire.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert to Government Equity all accounts payable which had remained outstanding in the books for a period of two (2) years and above pursuant to Section 98 of P.D. 1445 and DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 99-06 dated November 13, 1999. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to review all recorded payables and ascertain those without valid claims, revert the same and adjust the books accordingly. Instruct the Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books by excluding those obligations which are not valid or due and demandable for payment at year end in order to afford fair presentation on the accounts payable in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Not Implemented The tracing and review of accounts for the preparation of the schedule to be reverted back to Government Equity was still in process by the Accounting Office and the Municipal Accountant. Some accounts were already reverted.
observations.reason: Tracing and review were in process; some accounts already reverted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal accountant and municipal engineer verify the Construction in Progress accounts in coordination with the Municipal Accountant so that the completed projects can be transferred to the appropriate asset accounts and prepare the journal voucher transferring these completed projects to the appropriate asset account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant was still verifying the CIP accounts in coordination with the Municipal Engineer. The Accounting Office was preparing the necessary entries to transfer the completed projects to appropriate asset accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was still verifying prior year CIP accounts; necessary entries still being prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management reduce as much as possible their expenditures so that risk of an unhealthy financial position can be prevented. The Local Chief Executive should initiate the review of operations of the economic enterprises and implement strategies to revitalize the operations of the waterworks system so that it can finally become a viable economic enterprise as it was meant to be.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2009 Not Implemented The Office of the Municipal Treasurer conducted efforts to collect past due accounts and the waterworks office conducted disconnection for delinquent consumers. The municipal treasurer reported collections. The water district had yet to be established.
observations.reason: Efforts to collect delinquent accounts and disconnections were ongoing; establishment of water district pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor prioritize the filing of the application for titling of all real properties acquired by the municipal government so that transfer of certificates of title in favor of the Municipality can be issued to establish ownership thereto. The application was already sent to the DENR.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2007 Not Implemented The necessary legal documents were already gathered and the application for titling of the parcels was already sent to the DENR.
observations.reason: Preparation of documents completed; titling process pending with DENR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile the cashbooks with the corresponding subsidiary ledgers and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the cash balance as of December 31, 2007.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2007 Not Implemented Bank reconciliations were now being prepared and reconciling items were being taken up in the same manner that these items are also taken-up in ledger books so that current accounts are more or less reconciled.
observations.reason: Reconciling items still needed to be traced and verified to properly correct balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require all PHWs and Social Workers to support their claims for hazard pay with proof of actual exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks, certifications from the appropriate agencies and the documents required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in order to establish validity of payments.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the Mayor and the Municipal Health Officer will still refer the matter to their respective leagues.
observations.reason: the documents specified under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 were still not complete

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management henceforth stop the utilization of GAD funds for programs, projects and activities which do not clearly address gender issues and concerns, and instead endeavor to strictly comply with Section 36 of RA No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to ensure that the programs, projects, and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor asserted the importance of the grant of any fringe benefits and indicated the Municipality will still refer the matter to their respective leagues.
observations.reason: some utilizations were still included in the GAD Plan for CY 2020 and some utilizations were still made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal DRRMO prioritize the implementation of programs, projects and activities under the 70% Mitigation Fund which should be aligned with the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan of 2011-2028, in consideration of the indicators therein and to regularly monitor its implementation to ensure the municipal government's resilience to disaster.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer was also OIC General Services Officer, preventing him from focusing on the implementation of the PPAs of the fund.
observations.reason: the LDRRM Officer was also OIC General Services Officer, preventing focus on implementation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMF be reimbursed for the cost of 163 sacks of rice distributed to victims of two armed clashes between government troops and insurgents and to charge the expenses to the appropriations for Peace and Order. We further recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council determine whether all requisites and circumstances for the declaration of a state of calamity as required in R.A. No. 10121 and NDRRMC MC Order No. 60, s.2019 have been complied with and appropriate reports prepared to support their recommendation.
observations.managementAction: The concerned personnel were knowledgeable with the technical procedures and guidelines of RA 10121.
observations.reason: The procedural requirement of transferring the charges from LDRRMF to Peace and Order still needed to be made in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from using the 20% Development Fund for labor expenditures which are not among the allowable uses thereof as enumerated in the updated guidelines and instead, utilize the fund for priority development projects which are aligned with the Philippine Development Plan and the Public Investment Program, in accordance with DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. We further recommended that labor payrolls be supported with accomplishment reports and that the abovementioned expenditures be charged to the General Fund Proper pursuant to existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor believed in the importance of using the vehicles for constituents and noted these will be used for rescue purposes when disaster arises. The Municipal Accountant indicated not all labor payrolls were yet supported with Accomplishment Reports. The payroll for the construction of a Day Care Center still needs to be booked.
observations.reason: Not all labor payrolls were supported with Accomplishment Reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to establish and maintain a GAD database in order to provide the data necessary to properly plan GAD projects and activities. We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to create a gender-responsive Monitoring and Evaluation System in order that GAD projects and activities will be properly planned and implemented and its results assessed to ensure its success.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO also had to attend to more pressing issues due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Lacking skilled manpower to focus on the establishment of the GAD database and the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor and Municipal Treasurer request the treasurer of the respective leagues to acknowledge their payments (as well as collections from other member- municipalities) with government accountable form No. 51 in order that the funds may be subject to government accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor and the Vice Mayor will still refer the matter to their respective leagues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the use of the rescue vehicles be immediately stopped for ordinary, day-to-day operations in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles to times of actual calamity, and to subject them solely for rescue and response activities in conformity with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-115 dated December 17, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor believed in the importance of using the vehicles for constituents and that in times of disaster these will be prioritized for rescue purposes. For CY 2020, the vehicles were used during the COVID-19 pandemic to pick up Locally Stranded Individuals (LSIs) from ports of entry of Negros Oriental.
observations.reason: The vehicles were still used for ordinary operations during the pandemic situation

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that: a. The local chief executive immediately require the conduct of the physical inventory and to re-activate the team and re-visit the plan in order to continue whatever activities were initially undertaken. b. The accountant and treasurer maintain the ledger cards and property records for each real property and the respective property records in accordance with existing regulations. c. The results of the annual physical inventory be reconciled with the accounting and property records and the necessary adjustments made so that these records will agree with the actual count and the related accounts will thus be presented fairly in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Proper plans and concrete timetables regarding the conduct of the physical count of inventory have not been set yet. There have been movements of personnel causing the need for the inventory committee and the initial plan to continue whatever activities were initially undertaken.
observations.reason: There have been movements of personnel causing the need for the inventory committee; plans not yet set.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all employees concerned to refund the overpayment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive for CY 2014 and to henceforth observe strictly the applicable regulations on the grant of any fringe benefits.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was still awaiting the decision on the appeal filed related to the disallowance.
observations.reason: The Municipality was still awaiting decision on the appeal filed related to the disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LGU Management should evaluate and take into consideration the advantages in creating an Internal Audit Service, so that Management may achieve efficient and effective fiscal administration and performance of its affairs and functions.
observations.managementAction: Management still needed to create the Internal Audit Service (IAS) duly backed by budget allocation for the purpose.
observations.reason: Management still needed to create the Internal Audit Service (IAS) duly backed by budget allocation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. Management create an inventory committee to conduct the physical
count of inventories and properties, and that RPCI and RPCPPE be
submitted semestrally and annually, respectively;
b. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be required
to maintain and subsequently update the Ledger Cards and Stock and
Property Cards in order that the reconciliation of the physical count
against book balances can be facilitated; and
c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile the
accounting and property records and make the necessary adjustments
so that the accounts can be fairly presented in the financial statements.
In addition, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the
provisions of Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, specifically
charging of inventory to their appropriate expense accounts upon issuance
thereof.

We recommended that Management desist from charging ineligible
expenditures from BGCM, and to make sure that only expenses allowed under
DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 are charged thereto.

We recommended that Management properly determine the dates the
purchased food items were utilized, submit the daily monitoring sheets for those
quarantined for the identified period, and submit the daily menu for meals served
for persons under monitoring to the Audit Team in order for the latter to validate
the usage thereof. Moreover, we recommended that the end user and the BAC
adhere strictly to Section 7.3.2 (f) of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Management
a. render proper accounting of the difference noted from the purchased
and distributed food items;
b. install sound internal control mechanism in the repacking of goods to
monitor the release of items for repacking and account the packed
relief items to ensure that these are safeguarded from possible theft,
loss and wastage;
c. require the LDRRMO or property officer to prepare reports on the
inventory and monitoring of all procured and donated supplies,
materials, equipment, and relief goods in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; and
d. direct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies,
medicines, and relief goods for distribution in accordance with the
aforementioned COA Circulars.

We recommended that (a) the MSWDO submit a written explanation on
the lapses and deficiencies enumerated in this observation; and (b) henceforth,
require Management to establish a system in the distribution of SAP and similar
cash assistance programs wherein the guidelines issued by the DSWD are followed
and that proper validation and appropriate profiling of beneficiaries are
performed before disbursement.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned
personnel or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the Procuring Entity to
completely and properly fill up the relevant and required information in the PO
pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require end-user units to prepare their respective PPMPs in
accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 9184 using the suggested format
by the GPPB duly posted in its website.
b. Require the Budget Office to thoroughly evaluate and review the
submitted PPMPs in terms of amount and coherence with the proposed
budget of the LGU.
c. Require the BAC through the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the
PPMPs into a comprehensive, workable, and realistic APP, which will
serve as basis for procurement, in accordance to the applicable
provisions of RA No. 9184 using the prescribed format under GPPB
Circular No. 07-2015.
d. Submit the duly approved copy of the APP to the GPPB in accordance
with AO No. 46, series of 2015

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the BAC and the
BAC Secretariat to always indicate in the APP the appropriate mode of
procurement, and strictly comply with the procurement process mandated by the
IRR of R.A. No. 9184, and other related provisions of the GPPB
We also recommended and Management also agreed to submit the updated
or revised APP, in the prescribed format, duly approved and effecting the actual
method of procurement undertaken pursuant to the process, and the
documentary requirements as provided in GPPB Circular 01-2020 relative to
procurements under the Bayanihan Act for review and evaluation by the audit
team.

We recommended that Management advise the Municipal Budget Officer
to maintain the Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations. The
format of these forms can be referred to in Annexes 13, 14, 15 and 16 of Volume
II of the MNGAS. At the back of these forms are instructions on how it shall be
accomplished.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. immediately stop from utilizing the LDRRMF for PPAs which are not

observations.recommendation:
7.6 Recommendation:
7.6.1 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fast-track the preparation of the PPSAS-compliant CY 2016 financial statements and submit them to our office at the soonest possible time considering that these are long overdue. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management closely monitor invariable compliance to the submission of monthly and quarterly financial reports with complete schedules in accordance with the requirement of pertinent laws and regulations.

8.9 Recommendation:
8.9.1 We recommended the imposition of sanctions as provided under Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 specifically the filing of malversation charges and appropriate administrative proceedings on the erring accountable officers. We further recommended that henceforth, municipal officials who issue clearance, authorize and participate in the release of final amounts due officials who retire, die, separate or transfer see to it that unliquidated cash advances are deducted from their claims.

9.8 Recommendation:
9.8.1 We recommended that management evaluate whether the remaining unimplemented PPAs are still priority concerns and if so, should fast-track their implementation.

10.11 Recommendation:
10.11.1 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider limiting or reducing the number of job order workers to be hired to what is necessary for the implementation of projects such that the wages of these laborers will be correctly charged to the projects being implemented.

11.8 Recommendation:
11.8.1 We recommended that the municipality officially terminate the contract and take over the prosecution thereof by administration or award the same to a qualified contractor through negotiated contract pursuant to Section 8.4 of Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184. In addition, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee as well as the Municipal Accountant be required to explain why the contractor was allowed to proceed despite the absence of a performance bond and to collect advance payment, respectively, with an admonition that a repetition of the same be dealt with more severely in the future.

12.9 Recommendation:
12.9.1 We recommended that the Local School Board observe the limitations on the use of the Special Education Fund especially in the preparation of the budget and to limit charges thereto to purposes allowed under Section 272 of the Local Government Code and DECS –DBM –DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 1998.

13.7 Recommendation:
13.7.1 We recommended that the municipality observe caution in the use of the LCPC fund and strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 to ensure proper utilization thereof.

14.9 Recommendation:
14.9.1 We recommended that the municipal vice mayor and municipal mayor, as representatives of the municipality of Manjuyod, request the treasurer of the respective leagues to acknowledge their payments (as well as collections from other member-municipalities) with government accountable form No. 51 in order that the funds may be subject to government accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

15.7 Recommendation:
15.7.1 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor determine the person responsible for the renewal of motor vehicle registration and require him/her to refund the penalty charged to government funds.

16.8 Recommendation:
16.8.1 We recommended that the use of the rescue vehicles for ordinary, day-to-day operations be immediately stopped in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles to times of actual calamity solely for rescue and response activities in conformity with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014.

17.8 Recommendation:
17.8.1 We recommended that the management prioritize the passage of the municipal ordinance on solid waste management, the funding thereof and the assignment of enough personnel to handle the program.

18.9 Recommendation:
18.9.1 We recommended that management give priority to the implementation of GAD projects especially those which were not implemented in CY 2016 in order that the needs of women and the objectives of the program will be achieved.

We recommended that management closely monitor and vigorously pursue the timely and full implementation of all the programs and activities under the 20% development fund for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries. We further recommend that management should review the unimplemented programs, projects and activities, and propose, if warranted, realignment.
observations.managementAction: On-going implementation of the projects.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer said that the delay in the implementation of the project was due to the delayed approval of the budget and that they do not have the vehicles to haul materials to the project site. He further added that this year, they will implement the projects by contract to ensure prompt implementation.
